content.problemStatement:
As you have probably realized up to now that Devu is not a normal guy, he is a very weird and abnormal guy. Normally people have two hands, but Devu has three of them. So he wears three wrist watches on his hands. 



Devu loves to write name of his friends on his wrist watches. So he want to attach a string corresponding to name of his friends on each wrist watch. He is a quite particular about natural beauty, so he will not attach strings such that one of the string on a watch is prefix (not necessarily proper prefix) of some other string on other watch. Also alphabet size of characters in the string is equal to first **K** English Alphabets. (i.e. 1 ≤ **K ≤ 26**).



Now Devu wonders in how many ways he can select three non empty strings of length less than or equal to **N** to attach with his wrist watches. As answer could be very large, he wants you to print answer modulo **10^9 + 7**.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each test case consists of two integers N and K, where N is the maximum length of the strings and K is the size of the alphabet.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output the number of ways to select three non-empty strings of length less than or equal to N, such that no string is a prefix of another string, modulo 10^9 + 7.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Explanation: For the first test case, there are no valid strings because at least one string will be equal to another. For the second test case, there are 6 possible arrangements of the strings to the watches. For the third test case, there are 36 possible arrangements of the strings to the watches.

solution.approach: To solve this problem, we can use dynamic programming to calculate the number of valid strings of each length. We need to consider the number of ways to append a character to a string without creating a prefix of another string. We also need to handle the case where a string is a prefix of another string. The time complexity is O(T * N * K) and the space complexity is O(N * K).
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N * K) — we iterate over each test case, each possible length, and each possible character
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N * K) — we store the number of valid strings of each length and character
